You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 315e75d0).

## Issue to Resolve

experimental:function-return-type-spacing can violate max-line rule
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already be reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Steps to Reproduce
<!--- Provide a code example, or an unambiguous set of steps to -->
<!--- reproduce this bug. Minimize the example to the bare -->
<!--- minimum required to reproduce the bug. Small, well written -->
<!--- example are proven to be resolved quicker. -->

```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
        AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

will reformat to:
```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction(): AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

Which will violate the max-line-length rule.

The rule may need to make an exception for parameterless functions, or honor the max line length.

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used:
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
```
[*.{kt,kts}]
max_line_length = 120
```
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): ktlint 0.48.1, CLI

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
 4→import mu.KotlinLogging
 5→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 6→
 7→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE = 100 // https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#line_wrapping
 8→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE = 140
 9→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG = "off"
10→
11→/**
12→ * Integer value that denotes that the property is to be considered as disabled.
13→ */
14→public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE
15→
16→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
17→private var isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = false
18→
19→public val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
20→    EditorConfigProperty(
21→        name = PropertyType.max_line_length.name,
22→        type = PropertyType.max_line_length,
23→        defaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
24→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE,
25→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
26→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE,
27→        propertyMapper = { property, codeStyleValue ->
28→            when {
29→                property == null || property.isUnset -> {
30→                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
31→                }
32→
33→                /**
34→                 * Internally, Ktlint uses integer 'Int.MAX_VALUE' to indicate that the max line length has to be ignored as this is easier
35→                 * in comparisons to check whether the maximum length of a line is exceeded.
36→                 */
37→                property.sourceValue == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
38→
39→                else ->
40→                    PropertyType
41→                        .max_line_length
42→                        .parse(property.sourceValue)
43→                        .let {
44→                            if (!it.isValid) {
45→                                if (!isInvalidValueLoggedBefore) {
46→                                    isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = true
47→                                    LOGGER.warn { "Found invalid '.editorconfig' property value: ${it.errorMessage}" }
48→                                }
49→                                if (it.source == "-1") {
50→                                    MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
51→                                } else {
52→                                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
53→                                }
54→                            } else {
55→                                it.parsed
56→                            }
57→                        }
58→            }
59→        },
60→        propertyWriter = { property ->
61→            if (property <= 0 || property == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
62→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG
63→            } else {
64→                property.toString()
65→            }
66→        },
67→    )
68→
69→private fun CodeStyleValue.defaultValue() =
70→    when (this) {
71→        CodeStyleValue.android -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
72→        CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
73→        CodeStyleValue.official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
74→        CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
75→        CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE
76→    }
77→

  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType

  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule.VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule.Mode.REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.column
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY

 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpace
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.lineIndent
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiWhiteSpace
 22→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.PsiWhiteSpaceImpl
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtContainerNode
 25→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtDoWhileExpression
 26→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtIfExpression
 27→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtWhileExpression
 28→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.children
 29→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.getStrictParentOfType
 30→
 31→/**
 32→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#method-call-formatting
 33→ *
 34→ * The rule is more aggressive in inserting newlines after arguments than mentioned in the styleguide:
 35→ * Each argument should be on a separate line if
 36→ * - at least one of the arguments is
 37→ * - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 38→ * in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
 39→ */
 40→public class ArgumentListWrappingRule :
 41→    StandardRule(
 42→        id = "argument-list-wrapping",
 43→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 44→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 45→                // ArgumentListWrapping should only be used in case after normal wrapping the max_line_length is still
 46→                // violated
 47→                ruleId = WRAPPING_RULE_ID,
 48→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 49→            ),
 50→        ),
 51→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 52→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 53→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 54→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 55→        ),
 56→    ) {
 57→    private var editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 58→
 59→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 60→
 61→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 62→        editorConfigIndent = IndentConfig(
 63→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 64→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 65→        )
 66→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 70→        node: ASTNode,
 71→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 72→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 73→    ) {
 74→        if (editorConfigIndent.disabled) {
 75→            return
 76→        }
 77→
 78→        if (node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST) {
 79→            if (needToWrapArgumentList(node)) {
 80→                val newIndentLevel = node.getNewIndentLevel()
 81→                node
 82→                    .children()
 83→                    .forEach { child -> wrapArgumentInList(newIndentLevel, child, emit, autoCorrect) }
 84→            }
 85→        }
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    private fun needToWrapArgumentList(node: ASTNode) =
 89→        if ( // skip when there are no arguments
 90→            node.firstChildNode?.treeNext?.elementType != ElementType.RPAR &&
 91→            // skip lambda arguments
 92→            node.treeParent?.elementType != ElementType.FUNCTION_LITERAL &&
 93→            // skip if number of arguments is big (we assume it with a magic number of 8)
 94→            node.children().count { it.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT } <= 8
 95→        ) {
 96→            // each argument should be on a separate line if
 97→            // - at least one of the arguments is
 98→            // - maxLineLength exceeded (and separating arguments with \n would actually help)
 99→            // in addition, "(" and ")" must be on separates line if any of the arguments are (otherwise on the same)
100→            node.textContainsIgnoringLambda('\n') || node.exceedsMaxLineLength()
101→        } else {
102→            false
103→        }
104→
105→    private fun ASTNode.exceedsMaxLineLength() = (column - 1 + textLength) > maxLineLength && !textContains('\n')
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.getNewIndentLevel(): Int {
108→        val currentIndentLevel = editorConfigIndent.indentLevelFrom(lineIndent())
109→        return when {
110→            // IDEA quirk:
111→            // generic<
112→            //     T,
113→            //     R>(
114→            //     1,
115→            //     2
116→            // )
117→            // instead of
118→            // generic<
119→            //     T,
120→            //     R>(
121→            //         1,
122→            //         2
123→            //     )
124→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && hasTypeArgumentListInFront() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
125→
126→            // IDEA quirk:
127→            // foo
128→            //     .bar = Baz(
129→            //     1,
130→            //     2
131→            // )
132→            // instead of
133→            // foo
134→            //     .bar = Baz(
135→            //         1,
136→            //         2
137→            //     )
138→            currentIndentLevel > 0 && isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression() -> currentIndentLevel - 1
139→
140→            else -> currentIndentLevel
141→        }.let {
142→            if (isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword()) {
143→                it + 1
144→            } else {
145→                it
146→            }
147→        }
148→    }
149→
150→    private fun wrapArgumentInList(
151→        newIndentLevel: Int,
152→        child: ASTNode,
153→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
154→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
155→    ) {
156→        val indent = "\n" + editorConfigIndent.indent.repeat(newIndentLevel)
157→        when (child.elementType) {
158→            ElementType.LPAR -> {
159→                val prevLeaf = child.prevLeaf()
160→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace && prevLeaf.textContains('\n')) {
161→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
162→                    if (autoCorrect) {
163→                        prevLeaf.delete()
164→                    }
165→                }
166→            }
167→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT,
168→            ElementType.RPAR,
169→            -> {
170→                // aiming for
171→                // ... LPAR
172→                // <line indent + indentSize> VALUE_PARAMETER...
173→                // <line indent> RPAR
174→                val intendedIndent = if (child.elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT) {
175→                    indent + editorConfigIndent.indent
176→                } else {
177→                    indent
178→                }
179→
180→                val prevLeaf = child.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine() ?: child.prevLeaf()
181→                if (prevLeaf is PsiWhiteSpace) {
182→                    if (prevLeaf.getText().contains("\n")) {
183→                        // The current child is already wrapped to a new line. Checking and fixing the
184→                        // correct size of the indent is the responsibility of the IndentationRule.
185→                        return
186→                    } else {
187→                        // The current child needs to be wrapped to a newline.
188→                        emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
189→                        if (autoCorrect) {
190→                            // The indentation is purely based on the previous leaf only. Note that in
191→                            // autoCorrect mode the indent rule, if enabled, runs after this rule and
192→                            // determines the final indentation. But if the indent rule is disabled then the
193→                            // indent of this rule is kept.
194→                            (prevLeaf as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(intendedIndent)
195→                        }
196→                    }
197→                } else {
198→                    // Insert a new whitespace element in order to wrap the current child to a new line.
199→                    emit(child.startOffset, errorMessage(child), true)
200→                    if (autoCorrect) {
201→                        child.treeParent.addChild(PsiWhiteSpaceImpl(intendedIndent), child)
202→                    }
203→                }
204→                // Indentation of child nodes need to be fixed by the IndentationRule.
205→            }
206→        }
207→    }
208→
209→    private fun errorMessage(node: ASTNode) =
210→        when (node.elementType) {
211→            ElementType.LPAR -> """Unnecessary newline before "(""""
212→            ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT ->
213→                "Argument should be on a separate line (unless all arguments can fit a single line)"
214→            ElementType.RPAR -> """Missing newline before ")""""
215→            else -> throw UnsupportedOperationException()
216→        }
217→
218→    private fun ASTNode.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char: Char): Boolean {
219→        return children().any { child ->
220→            val elementType = child.elementType
221→            elementType == ElementType.WHITE_SPACE && child.textContains(char) ||
222→                elementType == ElementType.COLLECTION_LITERAL_EXPRESSION && child.textContains(char) ||
223→                elementType == ElementType.VALUE_ARGUMENT && child.children().any { it.textContainsIgnoringLambda(char) }
224→        }
225→    }
226→
227→    private fun ASTNode.hasTypeArgumentListInFront(): Boolean =
228→        treeParent.children()
229→            .firstOrNull { it.elementType == ElementType.TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST }
230→            ?.children()
231→            ?.any { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == true
232→
233→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfDotQualifiedAssignmentExpression(): Boolean =
234→        treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == ElementType.BINARY_EXPRESSION &&
235→            treeParent?.treeParent?.children()?.find { it.elementType == ElementType.DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION } != null
236→
237→    private fun ASTNode.prevWhiteSpaceWithNewLine(): ASTNode? {
238→        var prev = prevLeaf()
239→        while (prev != null && (prev.isWhiteSpace() || prev.isPartOfComment())) {
240→            if (prev.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
241→                return prev
242→            }
243→            prev = prev.prevLeaf()
244→        }
245→        return null
246→    }
247→
248→    private fun ASTNode.isOnSameLineAsControlFlowKeyword(): Boolean {
249→        val containerNode = psi.getStrictParentOfType<KtContainerNode>() ?: return false
250→        if (containerNode.node.elementType == ELSE) return false
251→        val controlFlowKeyword = when (val parent = containerNode.parent) {
252→            is KtIfExpression -> parent.ifKeyword.node
253→            is KtWhileExpression -> parent.firstChild.node
254→            is KtDoWhileExpression -> parent.whileKeyword?.node
255→            else -> null
256→        } ?: return false
257→
258→        var prevLeaf = prevLeaf() ?: return false
259→        while (prevLeaf != controlFlowKeyword) {
260→            if (prevLeaf.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return false
261→            prevLeaf = prevLeaf.prevLeaf() ?: return false
262→        }
263→        return true
264→    }
265→}
266→
267→public val ARGUMENT_LIST_WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = ArgumentListWrappingRule().ruleId
268→

package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard

import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfigProperty

internal val STANDARD_RULE_ABOUT = Rule.About(
    maintainer = "KtLint",
    repositoryUrl = "https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint",
    issueTrackerUrl = "https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues",
)

/**
 * Standard rules can only be declared and instantiated in the 'ktlint-ruleset-standard'. Custom rule set providers or API consumers have
 * to extend the [Rule] class to define a custom rule.
 */
public open class StandardRule internal constructor(
    id: String,
    override val visitorModifiers: Set<VisitorModifier> = emptySet(),
    override val usesEditorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>> = emptySet(),
) : Rule(
    ruleId = RuleId("$STANDARD_RULE_SET_ID:$id"),
    visitorModifiers = visitorModifiers,
    usesEditorConfigProperties = usesEditorConfigProperties,
    about = STANDARD_RULE_ABOUT,
)

 64→import mu.KotlinLogging

 66→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.PsiComment
 67→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 68→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 69→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.TokenSet
 70→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtStringTemplateExpression
 71→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtSuperTypeList
 72→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.leaves
 73→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.psiUtil.siblings
 74→
 75→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 76→
 77→/**
 78→ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 79→ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 80→ *
 81→ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 82→ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 83→ * indentation rule is not run.
 84→ */
 85→public class WrappingRule :
 86→    StandardRule(
 87→        id = "wrapping",
 88→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 89→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 90→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 91→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 92→        ),
 93→    ) {
 94→    private var line = 1
 95→    private lateinit var indentConfig: IndentConfig
 96→    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 97→
 98→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 99→        line = 1
100→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
101→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
102→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
103→        )
104→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
105→    }
106→
107→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
108→        node: ASTNode,
109→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
110→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
111→    ) {
112→        when (node.elementType) {
113→            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
114→            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
115→            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
116→            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
117→            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
118→            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
119→            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
120→        }
121→    }
122→
123→    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
124→        node: ASTNode,
125→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
126→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
127→    ) {
128→        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
129→
130→        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
131→        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
132→            return
133→        }
134→        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
135→        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
136→        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
137→        val wrapBlock =
138→            when {
139→                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
140→                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
141→                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
142→                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
143→                }
144→                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
145→                    val startOfLine = node.prevLeaf {
146→                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
147→                    }
148→                    val endOfLine = node.nextLeaf {
149→                        it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() || (it.elementType == REGULAR_STRING_PART && it.text == "\n")
150→                    }
151→                    val line =
152→                        startOfLine
153→                            ?.leaves()
154→                            ?.takeWhile { it != endOfLine }
155→                            ?.joinToString(separator = "", prefix = startOfLine.text.removePrefix("\n")) { it.text }
156→                            .orEmpty()
157→                    line.length > maxLineLength
158→                }
159→                else -> false
160→            }
161→        if (wrapBlock) {
162→            startOfBlock
163→                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
164→                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
165→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
166→                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
167→                        autoCorrect,
168→                        emit,
169→                    )
170→                }
171→        }
172→    }
173→
174→    private fun rearrangeBlock(
175→        node: ASTNode,
176→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
177→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
178→    ) {
179→        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
180→        var newlineInBetween = false
181→        var parameterListInBetween = false
182→        var numberOfArgs = 0
183→        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
184→        // matching ), ] or }
185→        val r = node.nextSibling {
186→            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
187→            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
188→            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
189→            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
190→            if (isValueArgument) {
191→                numberOfArgs++
192→                firstArg = it
193→            }
194→            it.elementType == rElementType
195→        }!!
196→        if (
197→            !newlineInBetween ||
198→            // keep { p ->
199→            // }
200→            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
201→            // keep ({
202→            // }) and (object : C {
203→            // })
204→            (
205→                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
206→                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
207→                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
208→                )
209→        ) {
210→            return
211→        }
212→        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
213→            // keep:
214→            // for (foo in listOf(
215→            //     "foo-1",
216→            //     "foo-2"
217→            // )) { ... }
218→            // but reject:
219→            // for (
220→            //     foo in listOf(
221→            //         "foo-1",
222→            //         "foo-2"
223→            //     )
224→            // ) { ... }
225→            return
226→        }
227→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
228→                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
229→                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
230→                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
231→                    it.textLength > 0
232→            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
233→            // IDEA quirk:
234→            // if (true &&
235→            //     true
236→            // ) {
237→            // }
238→            // instead of
239→            // if (
240→            //     true &&
241→            //     true
242→            // ) {
243→            // }
244→            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
245→        ) {
246→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
247→        }
248→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
249→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.lineIndent())
250→        }
251→    }
252→
253→    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
254→        node: ASTNode,
255→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
256→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
257→    ) {
258→        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
259→        if (
260→            node.textContains('\n') &&
261→            entries.size > 1 &&
262→            // e.g.
263→            //
264→            // class A : B, C,
265→            //     D
266→            // or
267→            // class A : B, C({
268→            // }), D
269→            //
270→            // but not
271→            //
272→            // class A : B, C, D({
273→            // })
274→            !(
275→                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
276→                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
277→                )
278→        ) {
279→            // put space after :
280→            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
281→                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
282→                if (
283→                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
284→                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
285→                ) {
286→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
287→                }
288→            }
289→            // put entries on separate lines
290→            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
291→            for (c in node.children()) {
292→                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
293→                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
294→                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
295→                ) {
296→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
297→                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
298→                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
299→                        emit = emit,
300→                        indent = node.lineIndent(),
301→                    )
302→                }
303→            }
304→        }
305→    }
306→
307→    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
308→        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
309→            ?.isPartOfComment() == true
310→
311→    private fun rearrangeValueList(
312→        node: ASTNode,
313→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
314→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
315→    ) {
316→        for (c in node.children()) {
317→            val hasLineBreak = when (c.elementType) {
318→                VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
319→                VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
320→                else -> false
321→            }
322→            if (hasLineBreak) {
323→                // rearrange
324→                //
325→                // a, b, value(
326→                // ), c, d
327→                //
328→                // to
329→                //
330→                // a, b,
331→                // value(
332→                // ),
333→                // c, d
334→
335→                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
336→                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
337→                if (
338→                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
339→                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
340→                ) {
341→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
342→                }
343→                // insert \n after multi-line value
344→                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
345→                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
346→                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
347→                if (
348→                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
349→                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
350→                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
351→                    // value(
352→                    // ), // a comment
353→                    // c, d
354→                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
355→                ) {
356→                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
357→                }
358→            }
359→        }
360→    }
361→
362→    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
363→        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
364→            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
365→
366→    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
367→        node: ASTNode,
368→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
369→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
370→    ) {
371→        node
372→            .treeParent
373→            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
374→            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
375→            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
376→            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
377→            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
378→            ?.let {
379→                // rewriting
380→                // """
381→                //     text
382→                // _""".trimIndent()
383→                // to
384→                // """
385→                //     text
386→                // _
387→                // """.trimIndent()
388→                emit(
389→                    node.startOffset,
390→                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
391→                    true,
392→                )
393→                if (autoCorrect) {
394→                    node as LeafPsiElement
395→                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
396→                }
397→                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
398→            }
399→    }
400→
401→    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
402→        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
403→        // if token is in lTokenSet
404→        //     find matching rToken
405→        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
406→        // return false
407→        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
408→        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
409→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
410→            // special cases:
411→            // x = y.f({ z ->
412→            // })
413→            // x = y.f(0, 1,
414→            // 2, 3)
415→            lToken = lToken.prevLeaf { it.elementType in LTOKEN_SET || it == node }
416→        }
417→        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType in LTOKEN_SET) {
418→            val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[lToken.elementType]
419→            val rToken = lToken.nextSibling { it.elementType == rElementType }
420→            return rToken?.treeParent == lToken.treeParent
421→        }
422→        if (nextCodeSibling?.textContains('\n') == false) {
423→            return true
424→        }
425→        return false
426→    }
427→
428→    private fun rearrangeArrow(
429→        node: ASTNode,
430→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
431→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
432→    ) {
433→        val p = node.treeParent
434→        if (
435→            // check
436→            // `{ p -> ... }`
437→            // and
438→            // `when { m -> ... }`
439→            // only
440→            p.elementType.let { it != FUNCTION_LITERAL && it != WHEN_ENTRY } ||
441→            // ... and only if expression after -> spans multiple lines
442→            !p.textContains('\n') ||
443→            // permit
444→            // when {
445→            //     m -> 0 + d({
446→            //     })
447→            // }
448→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && mustBeFollowedByNewline(node)) ||
449→            // permit
450→            // when (this) {
451→            //     in 0x1F600..0x1F64F, // Emoticons
452→            //     0x200D // Zero-width Joiner
453→            //     -> true
454→            // }
455→            (p.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY && node.prevLeaf()?.textContains('\n') == true)
456→        ) {
457→            return
458→        }
459→        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
460→            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
461→        }
462→        val r = node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE } ?: return
463→        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
464→            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.lineIndent())
465→        }
466→    }
467→
468→    private fun requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
469→        node: ASTNode,
470→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
471→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
472→        indent: String,
473→    ) {
474→        emit(
475→            node.startOffset - 1,
476→            """Missing newline before "${node.text}"""",
477→            true,
478→        )
479→        LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + ((if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before ${node.text}") }
480→        if (autoCorrect) {
481→            node.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + indent)
482→        }
483→    }
484→
485→    private fun requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
486→        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired: ASTNode,
487→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
488→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
489→        indent: String? = null,
490→        nodeToFix: ASTNode = nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired,
491→    ) {
492→        emit(
493→            nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.startOffset + 1,
494→            """Missing newline after "${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"""",
495→            true,
496→        )
497→        LOGGER.trace {
498→            "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline after ${nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired.text}"
499→        }
500→        if (autoCorrect) {
501→            val tempIndent = indent ?: (nodeToFix.lineIndent() + indentConfig.indent)
502→            nodeToFix.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe("\n" + tempIndent)
503→        }
504→    }
505→
506→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isMultiLine(): Boolean {
507→        for (child in node.children()) {
508→            if (child.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) {
509→                val v = child.text
510→                if (v == "\n") {
511→                    return true
512→                }
513→            }
514→        }
515→        return false
516→    }
517→
518→    private fun ASTNode.hasLineBreak(vararg ignoreElementTypes: IElementType): Boolean {
519→        if (isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) return true
520→        return if (ignoreElementTypes.isEmpty()) {
521→            textContains('\n')
522→        } else {
523→            elementType !in ignoreElementTypes &&
524→                children().any { c -> c.textContains('\n') && c.elementType !in ignoreElementTypes }
525→        }
526→    }
527→
528→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimIndent() = isFollowedBy("trimIndent()")
529→
530→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedByTrimMargin() = isFollowedBy("trimMargin()")
531→
532→    private fun KtStringTemplateExpression.isFollowedBy(callExpressionName: String) =
533→        this.node.nextSibling { it.elementType != DOT }
534→            .let { it?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION && it.text == callExpressionName }
535→
536→    /**
537→     *  Allow for-statement in which only the expression contains a newline:
538→     *     for (foo in listOf(
539→     *         "foo-1",
540→     *         "foo-2"
541→     *     )) { ... }
542→     * but reject:
543→     *     for (
544→     *         foo in listOf(
545→     *             "foo-1",
546→     *             "foo-2"
547→     *         )
548→     *     ) { ... }
549→     */
550→    private fun ASTNode.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression(): Boolean {
551→        if (treeParent.elementType != ElementType.FOR) {
552→            return false
553→        }
554→        if (this.elementType != LPAR) {
555→            return treeParent.findChildByType(LPAR)!!.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()
556→        }
557→        require(elementType == LPAR) {
558→            "Node should be the LPAR of the FOR loop"
559→        }
560→
561→        var node: ASTNode? = this
562→        while (node != null && node.elementType != RPAR) {
563→            if (node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
564→                return false
565→            }
566→            node = node.nextSibling { true }
567→        }
568→        return true
569→    }
570→
571→    override fun afterVisitChildNodes(
572→        node: ASTNode,
573→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
574→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
575→    ) {
576→        if (node.elementType == BLOCK) {
577→            val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
578→            if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
579→                return
580→            }
581→            val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
582→            val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
583→            val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
584→            val wrapBlock =
585→                !blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline && (
586→                    blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline || node.textContains('\n')
587→                    )
588→            if (wrapBlock && endOfBlock != null) {
589→                requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(
590→                    endOfBlock,
591→                    autoCorrect,
592→                    emit,
593→                    node.treeParent.lineIndent(),
594→                )
595→            }
596→        }
597→    }
598→
599→    private companion object {
600→        private val LTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(LPAR, LBRACE, LBRACKET, LT)
601→        private val RTOKEN_SET = TokenSet.create(RPAR, RBRACE, RBRACKET, GT)
602→        private val MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP =
603→            LTOKEN_SET.types.zip(
604→                RTOKEN_SET.types,
605→            ).toMap()
606→    }
607→}
608→
609→public val WRAPPING_RULE_ID: RuleId = WrappingRule().ruleId
610→

 114→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 115→
 116→public class IndentationRule :
 117→    StandardRule(
 118→        id = "indent",
 119→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 120→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 121→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 122→                ruleId = FUNCTION_SIGNATURE_RULE_ID,
 123→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 124→            ),
 125→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 126→                ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_CALL_SITE_RULE_ID,
 127→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 128→            ),
 129→            VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule(
 130→                ruleId = TRAILING_COMMA_ON_DECLARATION_SITE_RULE_ID,
 131→                mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
 132→            ),
 133→        ),
 134→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 135→            CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 136→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 137→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 138→        ),
 139→    ) {
 140→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 141→    private var indentConfig = IndentConfig.DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 142→
 143→    private var line = 1
 144→
 145→    private val indentContextStack: Deque<IndentContext> = LinkedList()
 146→
 147→    private lateinit var stringTemplateIndenter: StringTemplateIndenter
 148→
 149→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 150→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 151→        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 152→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 153→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 154→        )
 155→        if (indentConfig.disabled) {
 156→            stopTraversalOfAST()
 157→        }
 158→    }
 159→
 160→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 161→        node: ASTNode,
 162→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 163→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 164→    ) {
 165→        if (node.isRoot()) {
 166→            // File should not start with a whitespace
 167→            node
 168→                .nextLeaf()
 169→                ?.takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
 170→                ?.let { whitespaceWithoutNewline ->
 171→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Unexpected indentation", true)
 172→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 173→                        whitespaceWithoutNewline.treeParent.removeChild(whitespaceWithoutNewline)
 174→                    }
 175→                }
 176→            indentContextStack.addLast(startNoIndentZone(node))
 177→        }
 178→
 179→        when {
 180→            node.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() -> {
 181→                line++
 182→                if (indentContextStack.peekLast()?.activated == false) {
 183→                    val lastIndentContext = indentContextStack.removeLast()
 184→                    indentContextStack.addLast(
 185→                        lastIndentContext.copy(activated = true),
 186→                    )
 187→                }
 188→                visitNewLineIndentation(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 189→            }
 190→
 191→            node.elementType == CLASS_BODY ||
 192→                node.elementType == CONTEXT_RECEIVER_LIST ||
 193→                node.elementType == LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY ||
 194→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY ||
 195→                node.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE ||
 196→                node.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST ->
 197→                startIndentContext(
 198→                    fromAstNode = node,
 199→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 200→                )
 201→
 202→            node.elementType == SECONDARY_CONSTRUCTOR ->
 203→                visitSecondaryConstructor(node)
 204→
 205→            node.elementType == PARENTHESIZED &&
 206→                node.treeParent.treeParent.elementType != IF ->
 207→                startIndentContext(node)
 208→
 209→            node.elementType == BINARY_WITH_TYPE ||
 210→                node.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 211→                node.elementType == TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST ||
 212→                node.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST ->
 213→                startIndentContext(node)
 214→
 215→            node.elementType == DELEGATED_SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY ||
 216→                node.elementType == ANNOTATED_EXPRESSION ||
 217→                node.elementType == TYPE_REFERENCE ->
 218→                startIndentContext(
 219→                    fromAstNode = node,
 220→                    childIndent = "",
 221→                )
 222→
 223→            node.elementType == IF ->
 224→                visitIf(node)
 225→
 226→            node.elementType == LBRACE ->
 227→                visitLbrace(node)
 228→
 229→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST &&
 230→                node.treeParent.elementType != FUNCTION_LITERAL ->
 231→                startIndentContext(
 232→                    fromAstNode = node,
 233→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 234→                )
 235→
 236→            node.elementType == LPAR &&
 237→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CONDITION ->
 238→                visitLparBeforeCondition(node)
 239→
 240→            node.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER ->
 241→                visitValueParameter(node)
 242→
 243→            node.elementType == FUN ->
 244→                visitFun(node)
 245→
 246→            node.elementType == CLASS ->
 247→                visitClass(node)
 248→
 249→            node.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION ->
 250→                visitObjectDeclaration(node)
 251→
 252→            node.elementType == BINARY_EXPRESSION ->
 253→                visitBinaryExpression(node)
 254→
 255→            node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION ||
 256→                node.elementType == SAFE_ACCESS_EXPRESSION ||
 257→                node.elementType == USER_TYPE -> {
 258→                if (codeStyle == ktlint_official &&
 259→                    node.elementType == DOT_QUALIFIED_EXPRESSION &&
 260→                    node.treeParent?.elementType == ARRAY_ACCESS_EXPRESSION &&
 261→                    node.treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == CALL_EXPRESSION
 262→                ) {
 263→                    // Issue 1540: Deviate and fix from incorrect formatting in IntelliJ IDEA formatting and produce following:
 264→                    // val fooBar2 = foo
 265→                    //    .bar[0] {
 266→                    //        "foobar"
 267→                    //    }
 268→                    startIndentContext(
 269→                        fromAstNode = node.treeParent,
 270→                        toAstNode = node.treeParent.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 271→                    )
 272→                } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != node.elementType) {
 273→                    startIndentContext(node)
 274→                }
 275→            }
 276→
 277→            node.elementType == IDENTIFIER &&
 278→                node.treeParent.elementType == PROPERTY ->
 279→                visitIdentifierInProperty(node)
 280→
 281→            node.elementType == LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY &&
 282→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == CLOSING_QUOTE ->
 283→                visitWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 284→
 285→            node.elementType == WHEN ->
 286→                visitWhen(node)
 287→
 288→            node.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY ->
 289→                visitWhenEntry(node)
 290→
 291→            node.elementType == WHERE_KEYWORD &&
 292→                node.nextCodeSibling()?.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST ->
 293→                visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node)
 294→
 295→            node.elementType == KDOC ->
 296→                visitKdoc(node)
 297→
 298→            node.elementType == PROPERTY_ACCESSOR ->
 299→                visitPropertyAccessor(node)
 300→
 301→            node.elementType == FOR ||
 302→                node.elementType == WHILE ->
 303→                visitConditionalLoop(node)
 304→
 305→            node.elementType == LBRACKET ->
 306→                visitLBracket(node)
 307→
 308→            node.elementType == NULLABLE_TYPE ->
 309→                visitNullableType(node)
 310→
 311→            node.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION -> visitDestructuringDeclaration(node)
 312→
 313→            else -> {
 314→                LOGGER.trace { "No processing for ${node.elementType}: ${node.textWithEscapedTabAndNewline()}" }
 315→            }
 316→        }
 317→    }
 318→
 319→    private fun visitSecondaryConstructor(node: ASTNode) {
 320→        node
 321→            .findChildByType(CONSTRUCTOR_DELEGATION_CALL)
 322→            ?.let { constructorDelegationCall ->
 323→                val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 324→                val nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 325→                    fromAstNode = constructorDelegationCall,
 326→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 327→
 328→                // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 329→                if (fromAstNode != node.nextLeaf()) {
 330→                    startIndentContext(
 331→                        fromAstNode = node,
 332→                        toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 333→                        childIndent = "",
 334→                    )
 335→                }
 336→            }
 337→    }
 338→
 339→    private fun visitIf(node: ASTNode) {
 340→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 341→        node
 342→            .findChildByType(ELSE)
 343→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 344→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 345→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 346→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 347→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 348→            }
 349→
 350→        node
 351→            .findChildByType(THEN)
 352→            ?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 353→            ?.nextLeaf()
 354→            ?.let { nodeAfterThenBlock ->
 355→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 356→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterThenBlock,
 357→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 358→                    childIndent = "",
 359→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 360→            }
 361→        node
 362→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 363→            ?.nextCodeLeaf()
 364→            ?.let { nodeAfterConditionBlock ->
 365→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 366→                    fromAstNode = nodeAfterConditionBlock,
 367→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 368→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 369→            }
 370→        startIndentContext(
 371→            fromAstNode = node,
 372→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 373→            lastChildIndent = "", // No indent for the RPAR
 374→        )
 375→    }
 376→
 377→    private fun visitLbrace(node: ASTNode) {
 378→        // Outer indent context
 379→        val rbrace = requireNotNull(
 380→            node.nextSibling { it.elementType == RBRACE },
 381→        ) { "Can not find matching rbrace" }
 382→        startIndentContext(
 383→            fromAstNode = node,
 384→            toAstNode = rbrace,
 385→            firstChildIndent = "",
 386→            lastChildIndent = "",
 387→        )
 388→
 389→        // Inner indent context in reversed order
 390→        node
 391→            .treeParent
 392→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == FUNCTION_LITERAL }
 393→            ?.findChildByType(ARROW)
 394→            ?.let { arrow ->
 395→                startIndentContext(
 396→                    fromAstNode = arrow,
 397→                    toAstNode = rbrace,
 398→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 399→                )
 400→                startIndentContext(
 401→                    fromAstNode = node,
 402→                    toAstNode = arrow.prevCodeLeaf()!!,
 403→                    childIndent = arrow.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters(),
 404→                )
 405→            }
 406→    }
 407→
 408→    private fun ASTNode.calculateIndentOfFunctionLiteralParameters() =
 409→        if (codeStyle == ktlint_official || isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine()) {
 410→            // val fieldExample =
 411→            //      LongNameClass {
 412→            //              paramA,
 413→            //              paramB,
 414→            //              paramC ->
 415→            //          ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 416→            //      }
 417→            indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 418→        } else {
 419→            // Allow default IntelliJ IDEA formatting:
 420→            // val fieldExample =
 421→            //     LongNameClass { paramA,
 422→            //                     paramB,
 423→            //                     paramC ->
 424→            //         ClassB(paramA, paramB, paramC)
 425→            //     }
 426→            parent(CALL_EXPRESSION)
 427→                ?.let { callExpression ->
 428→                    val textBeforeFirstParameter =
 429→                        callExpression.findChildByType(REFERENCE_EXPRESSION)?.text +
 430→                            " { "
 431→                    " ".repeat(textBeforeFirstParameter.length)
 432→                }
 433→                ?: indentConfig.indent.repeat(2)
 434→        }
 435→
 436→    private fun ASTNode.isFirstParameterOfFunctionLiteralPrecededByNewLine() =
 437→        parent(FUNCTION_LITERAL)
 438→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
 439→            ?.prevSibling { it.textContains('\n') } != null
 440→
 441→    private fun visitLparBeforeCondition(node: ASTNode) {
 442→        startIndentContext(
 443→            fromAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextLeaf()), // Allow to pickup whitespace before condition
 444→            toAstNode = requireNotNull(node.nextCodeSibling()).lastChildLeafOrSelf(), // Ignore whitespace after condition but before rpar
 445→            nodeIndent = currentIndent() + indentConfig.indent,
 446→            childIndent = "",
 447→        )
 448→    }
 449→
 450→    private fun visitValueParameter(node: ASTNode) {
 451→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 452→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 453→        node
 454→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 455→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 456→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 457→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 458→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 459→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 460→            }
 461→
 462→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as constructor itself
 463→        val fromAstNode = node.skipLeadingWhitespaceCommentsAndAnnotations()
 464→        if (fromAstNode != node.firstChildNode &&
 465→            node.prevSibling { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() } == null &&
 466→            node == node.treeParent.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER)
 467→        ) {
 468→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 469→                fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 470→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 471→            ).fromASTNode.prevLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpace() }!!
 472→        } else {
 473→            startIndentContext(
 474→                fromAstNode = node,
 475→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 476→                childIndent = "",
 477→            )
 478→        }
 479→    }
 480→
 481→    private fun visitFun(node: ASTNode) {
 482→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 483→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 484→        val eqOrBlock =
 485→            node.findChildByType(EQ)
 486→                ?: node.findChildByType(BLOCK)
 487→        eqOrBlock?.let {
 488→            nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 489→                fromAstNode = eqOrBlock,
 490→                toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 491→            ).prevCodeLeaf()
 492→        }
 493→
 494→        node
 495→            .findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)
 496→            ?.let { typeReference ->
 497→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 498→                    fromAstNode = typeReference.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 499→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 500→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 501→            }
 502→
 503→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as function itself
 504→        startIndentContext(
 505→            fromAstNode = node,
 506→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 507→            childIndent = "",
 508→        )
 509→    }
 510→
 511→    private fun visitClass(node: ASTNode) {
 512→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 513→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 514→        node
 515→            .findChildByType(WHERE_KEYWORD)
 516→            ?.let { where ->
 517→                val typeConstraintList =
 518→                    requireNotNull(
 519→                        where.nextCodeSibling(),
 520→                    ) { "Can not find code sibling after WHERE in CLASS" }
 521→                require(
 522→                    typeConstraintList.elementType == TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST,
 523→                ) { "Code sibling after WHERE in CLASS is not a TYPE_CONSTRAINT_LIST" }
 524→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 525→                    fromAstNode = where.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 526→                    toAstNode = typeConstraintList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 527→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 528→            }
 529→        node
 530→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 531→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 532→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 533→                    fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 534→                    toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 535→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 536→            }
 537→
 538→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 539→        startIndentContext(
 540→            fromAstNode = node,
 541→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 542→            childIndent = "",
 543→        )
 544→    }
 545→
 546→    private fun visitObjectDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 547→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 548→        var nextToAstNode: ASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 549→        node
 550→            .findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
 551→            ?.let { superTypeList ->
 552→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 553→                    fromAstNode = superTypeList.getPrecedingLeadingCommentsAndWhitespaces(),
 554→                    toAstNode = superTypeList.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 555→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 556→            }
 557→
 558→        // Leading annotations and comments should be indented at same level as class itself
 559→        startIndentContext(
 560→            fromAstNode = node,
 561→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 562→            childIndent = "",
 563→        )
 564→    }
 565→
 566→    private fun visitBinaryExpression(node: ASTNode) {
 567→        if (isPartOfBinaryExpressionWrappedInCondition(node)) {
 568→            // Complex binary expression are nested in such a way that the indent context of the condition
 569→            // wrapper is not the last node on the stack
 570→            val conditionIndentContext =
 571→                indentContextStack.last { it.fromASTNode.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION }
 572→            // Create new indent context for the remainder (operator and right-hand side) of the binary expression
 573→            startIndentContext(
 574→                fromAstNode = node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)!!,
 575→                toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 576→                nodeIndent = conditionIndentContext.nodeIndent,
 577→                childIndent = conditionIndentContext.childIndent,
 578→            )
 579→            startIndentContext(node.firstChildNode)
 580→        } else if (node.treeParent?.elementType != BINARY_EXPRESSION ||
 581→            node.findChildByType(OPERATION_REFERENCE)?.firstChildNode?.elementType == ELVIS
 582→        ) {
 583→            startIndentContext(node)
 584→        }
 585→    }
 586→
 587→    private fun visitIdentifierInProperty(node: ASTNode) {
 588→        startIndentContext(
 589→            fromAstNode = node,
 590→            toAstNode = node.treeParent.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 591→        )
 592→    }
 593→
 594→    private fun visitWhen(node: ASTNode) {
 595→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 596→        node
 597→            .findChildByType(LPAR)
 598→            ?.let { lpar ->
 599→                nextToASTNode = startIndentContext(
 600→                    fromAstNode = lpar,
 601→                    toAstNode = node.findChildByType(RPAR)!!,
 602→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 603→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 604→            }
 605→        startIndentContext(
 606→            fromAstNode = node,
 607→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 608→        )
 609→    }
 610→
 611→    private fun visitWhenEntry(node: ASTNode) {
 612→        node
 613→            .findChildByType(ARROW)
 614→            ?.let { arrow ->
 615→                val outerIndent =
 616→                    if (arrow.nextLeaf()?.elementType == BLOCK) {
 617→                        ""
 618→                    } else {
 619→                        indentConfig.indent
 620→                    }
 621→                startIndentContext(
 622→                    fromAstNode = arrow.nextLeaf()!!,
 623→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 624→                    firstChildIndent = outerIndent,
 625→                    lastChildIndent = outerIndent,
 626→                )
 627→                startIndentContext(
 628→                    fromAstNode = node,
 629→                    toAstNode = arrow,
 630→                    childIndent = "",
 631→                )
 632→            }
 633→    }
 634→
 635→    private fun visitWhereKeywordBeforeTypeConstraintList(node: ASTNode) {
 636→        startIndentContext(
 637→            fromAstNode = node,
 638→            toAstNode = node.nextCodeSibling()?.lastChildLeafOrSelf()!!,
 639→            childIndent = TYPE_CONSTRAINT_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 640→        )
 641→    }
 642→
 643→    private fun visitKdoc(node: ASTNode) {
 644→        node
 645→            .findChildByType(KDOC_START)
 646→            ?.nextLeaf()
 647→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 648→                startIndentContext(
 649→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 650→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 651→                    childIndent = KDOC_CONTINUATION_INDENT,
 652→                )
 653→            }
 654→    }

 656→    private fun visitPropertyAccessor(node: ASTNode) {
 657→        var nextToASTNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 658→        node
 659→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 660→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 661→                nextToASTNode = startIndentContext(
 662→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 663→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 664→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 665→            }
 666→        // No indent on preceding annotations and comments
 667→        startIndentContext(
 668→            fromAstNode = node,
 669→            toAstNode = nextToASTNode,
 670→            childIndent = "",
 671→        )
 672→    }

 674→    private fun visitConditionalLoop(node: ASTNode) {
 675→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 676→        node
 677→            .findChildByType(BODY)
 678→            ?.takeIf { it.nextCodeLeaf()?.elementType != LBRACE }
 679→            ?.let { rpar ->
 680→                startIndentContext(
 681→                    fromAstNode = rpar,
 682→                    toAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf(),
 683→                )
 684→            }
 685→        node
 686→            .findChildByType(CONDITION)
 687→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 688→                startIndentContext(fromAstNode)
 689→            }
 690→    }

 692→    private fun visitLBracket(node: ASTNode) {
 693→        node
 694→            .treeParent
 695→            .takeUnless {
 696→                // Should be resolved in IntelliJ IDEA default formatter:
 697→                // https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KTIJ-14859/Too-little-indentation-inside-the-brackets-in-multiple-annotations-with-the-same-target
 698→                it.elementType == ANNOTATION
 699→            }?.findChildByType(RBRACKET)
 700→            ?.let { rbracket ->
 701→                startIndentContext(
 702→                    fromAstNode = node,
 703→                    toAstNode = rbracket,
 704→                    firstChildIndent = "",
 705→                    lastChildIndent = "",
 706→                )
 707→            }
 708→    }

 710→    private fun visitNullableType(node: ASTNode) {
 711→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 712→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 713→        node
 714→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 715→            ?.let { fromAstNode ->
 716→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 717→                    fromAstNode = fromAstNode,
 718→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 719→                    childIndent = "",
 720→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 721→            }
 722→        startIndentContext(
 723→            fromAstNode = node,
 724→            toAstNode = nextToAstNode,

 728→    private fun visitDestructuringDeclaration(node: ASTNode) {
 729→        // Inner indent contexts in reversed order
 730→        var nextToAstNode = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
 731→        node
 732→            .findChildByType(EQ)
 733→            ?.let { eq ->
 734→                nextToAstNode = startIndentContext(
 735→                    fromAstNode = eq,
 736→                    toAstNode = nextToAstNode,
 737→                ).prevCodeLeaf()
 738→            }
 739→
 740→        node
 741→            .findChildByType(RPAR)
 742→            ?.let {
 743→                startIndentContext(

 746→                    lastChildIndent = "",
